description: Critical mistakes to avoid when using AI-Hydro tools - based on actual hallucination patterns detected in testing

pitfalls.title: NEVER Use Raw NWIS Field Names
pitfalls.explanation: AI-Hydro transforms NWIS raw field names to clean, CAMELS-style names. Using raw names causes KeyError.
pitfalls.auto_fix_rule: Replace all NWIS raw field patterns with clean equivalents

pitfalls.title: NEVER Use Raw GridMET Variable Names
pitfalls.explanation: Climate tools return CAMELS-style names with _mean suffix, not raw GridMET variable names

pitfalls.title: NEVER Use Generic Field Names
pitfalls.explanation: AI-Hydro uses specific, prefixed field names following CAMELS conventions

pitfalls.title: NEVER Assume Fields Exist Without Checking

common_assumptions.actual_field: USE SPECIFIC FIELDS: elev_mean, elev_std, etc.
USE SPECIFIC FIELDS: p_mean, pet_mean, etc.

pitfalls.prevention: ALWAYS check tool documentation's 'returns.fields' section before accessing ANY field

pitfalls.title: NEVER Try to Use AI-Hydro Tools as MCP Servers
forbidden_patterns: from ai_hydro.tools.watershed import delineate_watershed  # use MCP instead
from hydrocatch import anything  # hydrocatch is removed
correct_approach: delineate_watershed(gauge_id='01031500', workspace_dir='/path')  # call MCP tool directly
pitfalls.explanation: AI-Hydro tools are MCP tools. Call them directly as function calls — never import Python.

pitfalls.title: NEVER Import from 'hydrocatch' Module
pitfalls.explanation: The 'hydrocatch' module was DEPRECATED and REMOVED. All functionality is in 'ai_hydro.tools.*'

testing_evidence.root_cause: AI assuming tools return raw API field names instead of transformed names

prevention_checklist: ☐ Use exact field names from documentation
☐ Never guess field names based on external API knowledge
☐ Call MCP tools directly (e.g. delineate_watershed) — never import Python
☐ Never use execute_command for ai_hydro analysis
